title: High Efficiency Transformation Protocol using NEB 10-beta Competent E. coli (C3019I)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"8c68d","text":"This is the correct protocol if you are using the C3019I cells. If you are using the C3019H cells, please refer to this protocol.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":115,"length":13}],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"BB3E043A73D140F2994F4FAC8BB169B0","url":"High-Efficiency-Transformation-Protocol-using-NEB-imst2v?step=7"}}}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"f33s0","text":"Transformation Protocol Variables","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":33}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"et18f","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"a2atc","text":"Thawing: Cells are best thawed on ice and DNA added as soon as the last bit of ice in the tube disappears. Cells can also be thawed by hand, but warming above 0°C will decrease the transformation efficiency.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":7}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8uq29","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2a3a5","text":"Incubation of DNA with Cells on Ice: For maximum transformation efficiency, cells and DNA should be incubated together on ice for 30 minutes. Expect a 2-fold loss in transformation efficiency for every 10 minutes this step is shortened.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":35}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7jf17","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"kmn6","text":"Heat Shock: Both the temperature and the timing of the heat shock step are important and specific to the transformation volume and vessel. Using the transformation tube provided, 30 seconds at 42°C is optimal.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":10}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"u9ti","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"55cun","text":"Outgrowth: Outgrowth at 37°C for 1 hour is best for cell recovery and for expression of antibiotic resistance. SOC gives 2-fold higher transformation efficiency than LB medium; and incubation with shaking or rotating the tube gives 2-fold higher transformation efficiency than incubation without shaking.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":9}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5ekrr","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dofrt","text":"Plating: Selection plates can be used warm or cold, wet or dry without significantly affecting the transformation efficiency. However, warm, dry plates are easier to spread and allow for the most rapid colony formation.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":7}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7bs02","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ej6qb","text":"DNA Contaminants to Avoid","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":25}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4ol0f","text":"ContaminantRemoval MethodDetergentsEthanol precipitatePhenolExtract with chloroform and ethanol precipitateEthanol or IsopropanolDry pellet before resuspendingPEG*Column purify or phenol/chloroform extract and ethanol precipitateDNA binding proteins (e.g. Ligase)Column purify or phenol/chloroform extract and ethanol precipitate","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":25}],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":329}],"data":[]},{"key":"afhlk","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"auhcq","text":"* Ideally, DNA for transformation should be purified and resuspended in water or TE. However, up to 10 µl of DNA directly from a ligation mix can be used with only a two-fold loss of transformation efficiency. Where it is necessary to maximize the number of transformants (e.g. a library), a purification step, either a spin column or phenol/chloroform extraction and ethanol precipitation should be added.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"tables","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"cellsMeta":{"0_0":"","0_1":"","1_0":"","1_1":"","2_0":"","2_1":"","3_0":"","3_1":"","4_0":"","4_1":"","5_0":"","5_1":""},"colWidths":[null,null],"data":[["\u003cstrong\u003eContaminant\u003c/strong\u003e","\u003cstrong\u003eRemoval Method\u003c/strong\u003e"],["Detergents","Ethanol precipitate"],["Phenol","Extract with chloroform and ethanol precipitate"],["Ethanol or Isopropanol","Dry pellet before resuspending"],["PEG*","Column purify or phenol/chloroform extract and ethanol precipitate"],["DNA binding proteins (e.g. Ligase)","Column purify or phenol/chloroform extract and ethanol precipitate"]],"guid":"7D26D1D0457F11E9A24D69A8AA1320D3","legend":"","mergeCells":[],"rowHeights":[],"show_headers":true}}}}

steps: Thaw a tube of NEB 10-beta Competent E. coli cells on ice until the last ice crystals disappear. 00:10:00 Cells are best thawed on ice and DNA added as soon as the last bit of ice in the tube disappears. Cells can also be thawed by hand, but warming above 0°C will decrease the transformation efficiency. NEB 10-beta Competent E.coli (High Efficiency) - 6x0.2 ml New England Biolabs Catalog #C3019I
Mix gently and carefully pipette 50 µl of cells into a transformation tube on ice. undetermined NEB 10-beta Competent E.coli (High Efficiency) - 6x0.2 ml New England Biolabs Catalog #C3019I
Add 1-5 µl containing 1 pg-100 ng of plasmid DNA to the cell mixture.
Carefully flick the tube 4-5 times to mix cells and DNA. Do not vortex .

Place the mixture on ice for 30 minutes. Do not mix. 00:30:00 For maximum transformation efficiency, cells and DNA should be incubated together on ice for 30 minutes. Expect a 2-fold loss in transformation efficiency for every 10 minutes this step is shortened.

Heat shock at exactly 42°C for exactly 30 seconds. Do not mix. 00:00:30 Both the temperature and the timing of the heat shock step are important and specific to the transformation volume and vessel. Using the transformation tube provided, 30 seconds at 42°C is optimal.
Place on ice for 5 minutes. Do not mix. 00:05:00
Pipette 950 µl of room temperature SOC into the mixture. undetermined SOC Outgrowth Medium - 100 ml New England Biolabs Catalog #B9020S

Place at 37°C for 60 minutes., shaking vigorously (250 rpm) or rotating. 01:00:00 Outgrowth at 37°C for 1 hour is best for cell recovery and for expression of antibiotic resistance. Expect a 2-fold loss in transformation efficiency for every 15 minutes this step is shortened. SOC gives 2-fold higher transformation efficiency than LB medium; and incubation with shaking or rotating the tube gives 2-fold higher transformation efficiency than incubation without shaking.

Warm selection plates to 37°C. Selection plates can be used warm or cold, wet or dry without significantly affecting the transformation efficiency. However, warm, dry plates are easier to spread and allow for the most rapid colony formation.
Mix the cells thoroughly by flicking the tube and inverting. SOC Outgrowth Medium - 100 ml New England Biolabs Catalog #B9020S
Perform several 10-fold serial dilutions in SOC.
Spread 50-100 µl of each dilution onto a selection plate
Incubate overnight at 37°C 15:00:00 Alternatively, incubate at 30°C for 24-36 hours or 25°C for 48 hours.
